mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real state sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicate robust household demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% annually, or RSAFS and CPIAUCSL show zero correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in over 60% of expansion-phase observations, or the mechanism Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which systematically increases S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 2-year windows, or GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in over 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative alpha relative to the market benchmark, or PAYEMS and UNRATE diverge without impacting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential construction demand and reduced supply chain activity, which depresses near-term corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines coincide with positive equity returns in over 70% of late-cycle observations, or the signal information coefficient drops below zero.
